| MCP:Wjk2YY | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## Features
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ---
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## Quick Start
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```sh
| MCP:Wjk2YY | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:Wjk2YY | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```sh
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```sh
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | The script will:
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```sh
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```sh
| MCP:Wjk2YY | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:Wjk2YY | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | # Run the application
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```sh
| MCP:Wjk2YY | docker compose up -d
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ---
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## Project Structure
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── config/
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:Wjk2YY | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:Wjk2YY | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ---
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## API Usage
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ---
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ---
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:Wjk2YY | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ---
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```java
| MCP:Wjk2YY | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ---
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ## Idempotency
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:Wjk2YY | 
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```java
| MCP:Wjk2YY | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:Wjk2YY | @Idempotent
| MCP:Wjk2YY | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:Wjk2YY | ```
| MCP:Wjk2YY |

Now I have a thorough picture. Here's the full explanation:

---

## Security Architecture of the Spring Boot Template

This template implements a **layered security model** built on Spring Security with JWT-based stateless authentication, role-based access control, and token lifecycle management. Here's how all the pieces fit together.

---

### 1. JWT Token Service (`JwtService.java`)

The core token engine uses the **JJWT library** (io.jsonwebtoken) with HMAC-SHA signing. Configuration is externalized to `application.properties`:

| Property | Value (dev default) | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `security.jwt.secret-key` | Base64-encoded 128-char hex string | HMAC signing key |
| `security.jwt.expiration` | `86400000` (24 hours) | Access token lifetime |
| `security.jwt.refresh-expiration` | `604800000` (7 days) | Refresh token lifetime |
| `security.jwt.issuer` | `SpringTemplate` | JWT `iss` claim |
| `security.jwt.authorization-key` | Separate secret string | Required for role promotion endpoint |

**What it does:**
- **Generates** both access and refresh tokens using `Jwts.builder()` with subject = username, issuedAt, expiration, and issuer claims
- **Validates** tokens by verifying the username matches the `UserDetails` and the token hasn't expired
- **Extracts** claims (username via `Claims::getSubject`) using `Jwts.parser().verifyWith(secretKey)`
- The signing key is derived by Base64-decoding the secret key into an HMAC-SHA key via `Keys.hmacShaKeyFor()`

---

### 2. Role & Permission Model

#### Roles (`Role.java`)
Three roles with a clear hierarchy:

| Role | Permissions |
|---|---|
| **USER** | *None* (empty set — can only access public and "authenticated" endpoints) |
| **MANAGER** | `MANAGER_READ`, `MANAGER_UPDATE`, `MANAGER_DELETE`, `MANAGER_CREATE` |
| **ADMIN** | All 8 permissions (all `ADMIN_*` + all `MANAGER_*`) |

Each `Role.getAuthorities()` method returns a `List<SimpleGrantedAuthority>` containing both the specific permission strings (e.g., `admin:read`) **and** the role itself prefixed with `ROLE_` (e.g., `ROLE_ADMIN`). This dual approach enables both `hasRole()` and `hasAuthority()` checks.

#### Permissions (`Permission.java`)
Eight granular permissions mapped to `resource:action` strings:
- `admin:read`, `admin:update`, `admin:create`, `admin:delete`
- `management:read`, `management:update`, `management:create`, `management:delete`

---

### 3. Security Filter Chain (`SecurityChain.java`)

This is the central configuration that wires everything together. Key behaviors:

**Public (unauthenticated) endpoints:**
- `/auth/**` — all authentication endpoints (register, login, refresh, authorize)
- `/v3/api-docs/**`, `/swagger-ui/**`, `/webjars/**` — Swagger documentation
- `/actuator/**` — health/info/metrics (though health details are shown only when authorized)

**Role-based URL security (for `/management/**`):**
- Requires `ROLE_ADMIN` or `ROLE_MANAGER` **at the role level**
- Then enforces **HTTP-method-specific permissions**:
  - `GET` → `ADMIN_READ` or `MANAGER_READ`
  - `POST` → `ADMIN_CREATE` or `MANAGER_CREATE`
  - `PUT/PATCH` → `ADMIN_UPDATE` or `MANAGER_UPDATE`
  - `DELETE` → `ADMIN_DELETE` or `MANAGER_DELETE`

**Everything else** (including `/users/**`) → requires authentication (`anyRequest().authenticated()`), with further per-method restrictions enforced by `@PreAuthorize` annotations on controllers.

**Session management** is fully **STATELESS** — no server-side sessions, all state lives in the JWT.

**Logout** is configured at `/auth/logout`, which invokes `LogoutService` to revoke the token.

**CSRF is disabled** (appropriate for a pure API with JWT authentication).

---

### 4. JWT Authentication Filter (`AuthenticationFilter.java`)

This `OncePerRequestFilter` runs **before** `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter` on every request:

1. **Short-circuits** if the path contains `/api/v1/auth` (no token needed for auth endpoints)
2. **Extracts** the `Bearer` token from the `Authorization` header
3. **Parses** the username from the JWT via `JwtService`
4. **Validates** the token in two ways:
   - **Cryptographic validity** — `JwtService.isTokenValid()` checks signature + expiration + username match
   - **Database validity** — looks up the token in the `TOKEN` table and checks `!expired && !revoked`
5. **Sets** a `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken` in the `SecurityContextHolder` with the user's full authorities

This dual validation (JWT + database token registry) is crucial — it means tokens can be **revoked server-side** by marking them as expired/revoked in the database.

---

### 5. Token Entity & Database-Backed Revocation (`TokenEntity.java`, `TokenRepository`)

Every issued JWT is persisted to the `TOKEN` table with:
- `token` — the raw JWT string
- `type` — always `BEARER`
- `revoked` — boolean flag
- `expired` — boolean flag
- `user_id` — foreign key to `_USER` (cascade on delete)

The `TokenRepository` provides:
- `findByToken(String)` — used during filter validation
- `findAllValidTokenByUser(Long)` — used during logout/re-login to revoke all prior tokens

**On login**, all previously valid tokens for that user are revoked first, then the new one is saved. This ensures only the most recent login session is valid (single active session per user model).

---

### 6. Authentication Flow for Users

#### Registration: `POST /api/v1/auth/register`
```json
{
  "username": "john",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "name": "John Doe",
  "password": "secret123"
}
```
- Checks for duplicate username/email
- Password is hashed with **BCrypt** (`BCryptPasswordEncoder`)
- New user gets `Role.USER` by default
- Returns both `accessToken` and `refreshToken`
- The access token is saved in the `TOKEN` table
- Protected by **idempotency** (1-hour TTL, Redis-backed) and **rate limiting** (5 req/min — `AUTHENTICATION` category)

#### Login: `POST /api/v1/auth/login`
```json
{
  "username": "john",
  "password": "secret123"
}
```
- Authenticates via Spring's `AuthenticationManager` (which uses the `DaoAuthenticationProvider` with BCrypt)
- **Revokes all existing tokens** for the user, then issues fresh access + refresh tokens
- Returns `AuthenticationResponse` with both tokens

#### Token Refresh: `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh-token`
- Client sends the **refresh token** in the `Authorization: Bearer <refresh-token>` header
- If the refresh token is valid (not expired, signature correct, user exists), a new access token is generated
- Old tokens are revoked; new access token is saved

#### Logout: `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`
- Extracts the Bearer token, finds it in the database, marks it `expired` and `revoked`
- Clears the `SecurityContextHolder`

#### Role Authorization (Promotion): `POST /api/v1/auth/authorize`
- **Guarded** by `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN') || hasRole('USER') || hasRole('MODERATOR')")` — any authenticated user can call it, BUT...
- Requires a **secret key** in the request body (`authorizationKey` from properties) — this is a gatekeeping mechanism for role escalation
- Changes the user's role and issues new tokens

---

### 7. Secured Endpoint Interaction (`UserController.java`)

Users interact with the secured `/users/**` endpoints as follows:

| Endpoint | Access | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /users/current` | Any authenticated user | Returns the current user's profile |
| `PATCH /users/current` | Any authenticated user | Updates own profile |
| `POST /users/` | `ADMIN` only | Create user; rate-limited (20/min per user), idempotent |
| `GET /users/` | `ADMIN` only | List all users with pagination |
| `GET /users/{id}` | `ADMIN` only | Get user by UUID |
| `DELETE /users/{id}` | `ADMIN` only | Delete user |
| `PATCH /users/{id}` | `ADMIN` only | Update user by UUID |

Access is enforced at **two levels**:
1. **URL-level** in `SecurityChain` — any `/users/**` path requires authentication
2. **Method-level** via `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")` on each controller method

---

### 8. Unauthenticated Entry Point (`AuthenticationEntryPoint.java`)

When an unauthenticated request hits a protected endpoint, this component:
- Returns HTTP `401 Unauthorized`
- Produces a structured JSON response using `BaseResponse` with a `GenericErrorMessage` containing the path and error details
- Logs the event

---

### 9. Additional Security Layers

- **Rate Limiting**: Annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with 4 categories (strict/standard/relaxed/authentication), backed by Redis
- **Idempotency**: `@Idempotent` annotation prevents duplicate processing of mutating endpoints; clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header; Redis-backed deduplication
- **Password Storage**: BCrypt via `BCryptPasswordEncoder`
- **Correlation IDs**: `X-Correlation-ID` header propagated through MDC for distributed tracing

---

### Summary of the User Journey

```
1. Register    → POST /auth/register → get accessToken + refreshToken
2. Login       → POST /auth/login    → get new accessToken + refreshToken (old ones revoked)
3. Use API     → Any endpoint with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>
4. Self-manage → GET/PATCH /users/current (any authenticated user)
5. Admin ops   → CRUD /users/** (ADMIN role required, via @PreAuthorize)
6. Refresh     → POST /auth/refresh-token with old refreshToken → get new accessToken
7. Logout      → POST /auth/logout → token revoked in database, context cleared
```

The template provides a production-ready security foundation with defense-in-depth: JWT signature verification, database-backed token revocation, BCrypt passwords, granular role/permission enforcement, rate limiting, and idempotency protection.
